An expert system defined as a programming structure capable of storing and using knowledge about a given area that translates into its learning capacity. Similarly, AI can be considered as the ability of machines to use algorithms, learn from data and use what is learned in decision-making as a human being would. According to Takeyas (2007), AI is a branch of computer science charged with studying computational models capable of performing human activities based on two of their primary characteristics: reasoning and conduct. In 1956, John McCarthy coined the term ‘artificial intelligence’, and defined it as ‘the science and ingenuity of making intelligent machines, especially smart computing programs’. Grau-Luque contrasts different definitions from different sources and authors, noting that they differ depending on ‘in which specific field they are used’. This leads the author to define ‘artificial intelligence’ as ‘systems that carry out tasks considered intelligent’, and then to associate concepts such as ‘learning’ and ‘reason’ with automatic learning as a subdiscipline of artificial intelligence.

Some simple applications of natural language processing include information retrieval, text mining, answering questions, and machine translation. Many approaches use word frequencies to build syntactic text representations.
